query: Draft an email to support@datocms.com about the DatoCMS MCP getting stuck.
Use datocms-feedback to summarize the failed skills/MCP experience for support.
Help me report feedback to DatoCMS: the MCP kept looping and I gave up.
The DatoCMS skills kept misunderstanding my setup and I want to send support a summary.
This skill routed wrong three times; help me report feedback to DatoCMS.
This DatoCMS MCP flow has looped several times and I want DatoCMS to know what went wrong.
I am frustrated with how the DatoCMS setup skill keeps asking for the same thing; draft feedback I can email.
We have tried the same MCP update five times, it keeps returning to the same invalid payload, and I want a support email describing the loop.
The skills never converged after repeated wrong routing between setup and frontend integrations. Please write a concise report for DatoCMS support.
I'm done trying to make this MCP workflow work. Prepare a sanitized summary I can send to support.
The MCP call returned a validation error for a field ID; retry with the corrected field name.
The MCP says the 'category' field does not exist on article. Inspect the model fields and try the update again.
That API error tells us the enum must be draft or published. Use the allowed enum value and rerun the call.
The MCP returned an error that explains the allowed enum values; use that error message and continue.
The first update failed because the record is not published yet. Publish it first, then retry the same change.
The tool call failed once with a 422, but now we know the nested block payload shape. Fix the payload and keep going.
It errored because the environment is sandbox, not main. Switch the environment option and rerun.
The first migration failed because the model already exists; inspect the schema and adjust the migration.
The migration threw a duplicate field error. Rename the field in the migration and run it again.
npx datocms migrations:run failed because I am not logged in. Run login and then continue the migration workflow.
The generated migration targeted the wrong field API key. Inspect schema:inspect output and regenerate the patch.
The GraphQL query failed because the slug field is missing; inspect available fields and try again.
The GraphQL response says _allArticles does not accept that filter. Check the schema and rewrite the filter.
The query failed on the first try because draft mode needs includeDrafts. Add includeDrafts and keep going.
The script hit a missing DATOCMS_API_TOKEN value; add the env var and rerun it.
The setup recipe says DATOCMS_READONLY_TOKEN is missing. Add the placeholder to .env.example and continue wiring the query client.
The preview route returned 401 because the secret does not match. Update the local env value and retry the preview link.
This visual editing setup is broken and annoying; fix the overlay in this Next.js project.
Fix visual editing in this Next.js project; the overlay is not showing up.
This plugin UI looks bad; make the config screen match the DatoCMS dashboard.
This content model is frustrating; help me decide whether testimonials should be blocks or records.
This migration flow is driving me crazy, but keep debugging the failed release workflow and get it passing.
The MCP result is wrong, but do not give up: inspect the record shape and try a narrower update.
This is broken. Continue from the error message and make the import script work.
The upload failed because the remote URL timed out. Retry the asset upload with a local file stream.
The DatoCMS plugin hook failed because connect() does not expose that method. Inspect the SDK docs and patch the hook.
The scaffolded plugin fails to start because Vite cannot resolve the SDK. Fix dependencies and keep the scaffold small.
The Structured Text renderer throws on inline records. Inspect the allowed blocks and update the renderer.
The site search setup returned zero results. Check crawler config and keep debugging the search route.
I got an error from the setup skill saying it needs the project linked first. Link the project and continue setup.
